finding.contract_claim: The schema field `severity` declares an enum constraint (["error", "warning"]) — an obligation that consuming code must enforce when the value is provided.
finding.observed_behavior: No application code validates a `severity` configuration value against this enum; the declaration appears only inside an LLM tool_use input_schema in tools.py, where the model itself is the consumer of the constraint.

finding.triage_reasoning: Same reasoning as Claim 5. The `severity` enum `['error', 'warning']` is declared inside an LLM `tool_use` input_schema. The model is the consumer of this constraint; the schema guides the model to return one of the enumerated values. Application code does not need to re-validate the output against these bounds because the JSON Schema is the specification handed to the model. No actionable gap exists in application code.
